blockingIssues.description: The rerun still bypasses the shipped app shell by mounting `IdentitiesScreen`, `HostsScreen`, and `SessionsScreen` directly with `composeRule.setContent(...)` instead of exercising `MainActivity`/`AtermNavHost`, so it does not prove visible connect/trust/edit behavior through the real navigation path.

The supposed relaunch proof still swaps a fresh `AppContainer` into the same in-process Compose root rather than performing a real activity/app relaunch or restart path, so keystore-backed identity reuse after relaunch remains unproven.

The terminal boundary remains a Compose `LazyColumn` plus the repo's minimal `TerminalBuffer` instead of a dedicated terminal-emulator/view boundary, so representative full-screen/TUI behavior is still not trustworthy end to end.

The added tests still do not prove special keys, paste, viewport-driven resize, and representative full-screen behavior against the live SSH fixture; several assertions rely on fake controllers instead of a live SSH/PTY-backed session.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Explicitly forbid using `composeRule.setContent(...)` screen mounting and in-process `AppContainer` swapping as substitutes for real app-shell navigation and relaunch validation when a feature requires visible UI and restart proof.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `ssh-session-ui-trust-and-relaunch-proof` found the worker interacted with visible Compose screens but still bypassed `MainActivity`/`AtermNavHost` and simulated relaunch by swapping containers in-process, yet the handoff treated this as sufficient proof.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify exec-mode expectations for terminal validation: automation may substitute for manual interaction only when it still exercises a live SSH fixture, and special keys, paste, resize, and representative full-screen behavior must be proven against that live fixture rather than fake session controllers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `live-terminal-boundary-truthfulness-and-viewport-resize` found the worker fixed local echo and viewport sizing but still relied on test controllers instead of the live fixture for critical terminal-behavior proof, while the skill's current manual-check language was treated as flexible.
